senses.definition: a fast-paced, urban version of baseball played with a rubber ball and bare hands

examples.x: In Baseball5, players use their bare hands to catch and throw the ball.
The rules of Baseball5 allow for quick and dynamic gameplay.
Baseball5 is gaining popularity as a fun and accessible urban sport.
Unlike traditional baseball, Baseball5 doesn't require bats or gloves.
The simplicity of Baseball5 makes it easy for anyone to pick up and play.

senses.definition: a game similar to baseball but on a smaller field in which players use a larger and softer ball
a minor league team, especially in baseball, affiliated with a major league club where players develop before potentially joining the major league roster

examples.x: He was scouted by the major league team while playing for their farm team.
The farm team's success in developing young talent is crucial for the organization.
The pitcher spent two seasons with the farm team before making his debut in the majors.
They sent their top prospect down to the farm team to work on his hitting mechanics.
The farm team's game was attended by several scouts from different major league clubs.

senses.definition: a defensive play in which two outs are made by the fielding team in a single continuous play

examples.x: The shortstop and second baseman turned a double play to end the inning.
They executed a perfect 6-4-3 double play to thwart the rally.
His throw to first completed the double play.
The double play shifted the momentum in their favor.
The pitcher induced a ground ball double play to escape the jam.

senses.definition: a batter's turn to bat against the opposing pitcher during a game

examples.x: He had a productive at-bat with a double in the gap
The batter struck out in his first at-bat of the game.
She hit a home run in her second at-bat.
His at-bat ended with a line drive to center field.
The pitcher threw a wild pitch during the at-bat.

senses.definition: (baseball) a batting technique where a batter softly taps the ball with the bat to place it into play, often to advance a baserunner

examples.x: The batter attempted a bunt to move the runner to second base.
The pitcher fielded the bunt and threw to first base for the out.
The batter's bunt rolled perfectly down the third baseline for a hit.
The team used a bunt to manufacture a run in the late innings.

senses.definition: (baseball) a hit that travels high into the air and is caught by a fielder before it touches the ground

examples.x: The fly ball was caught for the third out of the inning.
The outfielder caught the fly ball near the warning track.
She misplayed the fly ball, allowing the batter to reach second base.
The fly ball sailed over the outfield fence for a home run.
The umpire signaled an infield fly ball with runners on first and second.

senses.definition: (baseball) a play where a fielder touches the base with the ball to get an out before the baserunner arrive

examples.x: The pitcher fielded a ground ball and threw to home plate for the force-out.
The shortstop made a quick throw to first base for the force-out.
The second baseman tagged second base for the force-out on the sliding runner.
The force-out at third base ended the inning with no runs scored.

senses.definition: (baseball) a baserunner's attempt to advance to the next base while the pitcher is preparing to pitch or during the pitch itself

examples.x: His steal of third base surprised the defense.
The catcher's throw prevented a potential steal.
She leads the team in steals this season.
The runner's successful steal put him in scoring position.
His steal of home plate was the highlight of the game.

senses.definition: (baseball) a situation where a defensive player forces a baserunner out by touching a base with the ball before the baserunner reaches that base

examples.x: The shortstop made a force play at second base to retire the lead runner.
The first baseman stepped on the bag for a force play to end the inning.
They executed a force play at home plate to prevent the run from scoring.
The force play at second base was a key out in the double play.
The force play at home plate ended the scoring threat.

senses.definition: (baseball) a hit that travels outside the foul lines, usually resulting in a strike against the batter

examples.x: He hit a foul ball that landed just beyond the dugout.
The umpire signaled a foul ball on the high fly to left field.
The batter hit three foul balls in a row before striking out.
The foul ball bounced off the netting behind home plate.
The pitcher induced a foul ball on the inside pitch.

senses.definition: (baseball) a hit that rolls along the ground, typically resulting in a play by the infielders to get the batter out

examples.x: The infielder fielded the ground ball cleanly and threw to first base.
He hit a hard ground ball past the diving shortstop.
The shortstop backhanded the ground ball and made a quick throw to first.
He chopped a ground ball to the third baseman, who made a strong throw to first.
The third baseman fielded the ground ball and fired to second for the force out.

senses.definition: to hit a ground ball that is fielded by the defense, resulting in an out

examples.x: He grounds out on a sharply hit ball to the third baseman.
He grounded out, leaving runners stranded on base.
The batter grounded out, advancing the runner to second base.

senses.definition: a hit in baseball that occurs when a batter hits the ball and runs around all four bases, scoring a run for their team

examples.x: The home run was the only score in the pitcher's duel.
The team celebrated after his walk-off home run in extra innings.
The crowd erupted as she smashed a grand slam home run.
His solo home run in the ninth inning tied the game.
The rookie surprised everyone with a tape-measure home run.

senses.definition: (baseball) a hard-hit ball that travels directly and quickly through the air, usually at a low angle, making it difficult for fielders to catch

examples.x: She hit a sharp line drive into the gap between left and center field.
The batter's line drive sailed over the shortstop's head for a base hit.
The outfielder made a diving catch to snag the line drive.
The line drive ricocheted off the pitcher's glove and into center field.
His line drive down the third baseline was ruled foul.

senses.definition: a hit by a backup batter in baseball

examples.x: The coach called for a pinch hit to shake things up in the lineup.
He delivered a crucial pinch hit when the team needed it most.
The pinch hit resulted in a game-changing RBI.
She was known for her clutch pinch hits in tight situations.
The pinch hit sparked a rally that led to a comeback victory.

senses.definition: a baseball pitch that the batter does not swing at and is not called a strike by the umpire because it misses the strike zone

examples.x: He took a close pitch that just missed the outside corner for a ball.
The umpire signaled a ball after the pitch sailed high above the strike zone.
The pitcher aimed for the corners but missed, resulting in a ball.
The batter disciplined himself not to swing at the pitcher's deceptive changeup that dropped low for a ball.

senses.definition: (baseball) a pitch that the batter swings at and misses, or that the umpire calls within the strike zone

examples.x: The umpire called a strike on the inside corner of the plate.
The count is now 0-2 after two consecutive strikes.
He took a strike on the outside part of the plate.
The batter couldn't hold back on the swing and got called out on strikes.
The umpire signaled a strike after the batter didn't swing at a breaking ball.

senses.definition: an out that happens when a batter receives three strikes during their turn at bat

examples.x: He avoided a strikeout by fouling off several tough pitches.
The pitcher's fastball had so much movement that it led to a strikeout.
She led the league in strikeouts last season due to her powerful pitching.
His strikeout rate improved significantly after adjusting his swing mechanics.
The rookie pitcher impressed everyone with a strikeout on his first major league pitch.

senses.definition: (baseball) a batted ball hit high into the air but not far, typically resulting in an easy catch for an outfielder or infielder

examples.x: She lifted a pop fly to right field that the outfielder caught on the run.
He fouled off a pitch that turned into a pop fly caught by the first baseman.
The pop fly was caught by the second baseman in center field.
The outfielder settled under the pop fly near the foul line for the catch.
The pitcher induced a weak pop fly to the shortstop for the second out.

senses.definition: (baseball) a statistic that credits a batter when their hit or action allows a runner to score

examples.x: The rookie's first major league hit was a double that earned him his first run batted in.
Despite not getting a hit, his fielder's choice resulted in a run batted in.

senses.definition: (baseball) the base where the batter stands and where players must reach to score a run

examples.x: He slid into home plate just before the tag, scoring a run.
The umpire called him safe as he crossed home plate.
She hit a home run and touched home plate to complete the score.
The runner dashed from third base to home plate on a wild pitch.

senses.definition: (baseball) the fence or wall behind home plate that stops wild pitches and protects spectators

examples.x: He retrieved the ball quickly after it hit the backstop.
The ball bounced off the backstop after the wild pitch.
The catcher missed the ball, but it was stopped by the backstop.
They installed a new backstop to improve safety.
The pitcher threw a wild pitch that rebounded off the backstop.

senses.definition: (baseball) a pitch thrown close to a batter to intimidate or force them away from the plate

examples.x: The batter felt the tension rise as the pitcher delivered a sharp brushback pitch.
The batter glared at the pitcher after narrowly dodging a brushback pitch.
A brushback pitch can send a message to aggressive hitters.
Sometimes a brushback pitch is used strategically to keep hitters off balance.
The crowd reacted with a mix of gasps and cheers as the pitcher threw a high brushback pitch.

senses.definition: (baseball) a type of pitch that bends or curves as it approaches the batter

examples.x: The pitcher threw a curve that fooled the batter completely.
The batter was ready for the fastball but got a curve instead.
Her curve dropped sharply, making it hard to hit.
He struck out swinging at a nasty curve.
The pitcher relied on his curve to get out of tough situations.

senses.definition: (baseball) a type of pitch thrown at high speed with minimal movement

examples.x: The pitcher’s fastball clocked in at 95 mph.
He struck out three batters with his blazing fastball.
She threw a fastball right down the middle for a strike.
The batter couldn't catch up to the high fastball.
Her fastball overpowered the hitter, resulting in a swing and miss.

senses.definition: (baseball) a pitch thrown with a grip that causes the ball to drop sharply before reaching the plate

examples.x: The pitcher surprised the batter with a devastating forkball.
The pitcher's forkball dipped just below the hitter's bat.
When thrown correctly, the forkball can be a pitcher's most effective weapon.
Learning to control the movement of the forkball takes time and practice.
Hitters often struggle to make solid contact with a well-executed forkball.

senses.definition: a deliberate pitch thrown by the pitcher far outside the strike zone in baseball

examples.x: With a runner on first, the team decided to call for a pitchout to prevent a stolen base.
The catcher's accurate throw after the pitchout caught the baserunner by surprise.
The catcher's quick reflexes enabled him to make a strong throw after the pitchout.
Coaches often use pitchouts strategically to control the opponent's running game.
The pitcher executed a perfect pitchout, giving the catcher a clear shot at the runner.

senses.definition: a relief pitcher successfully preserving a lead to win the baseball game

examples.x: The closer secured the save with a final strikeout.
The reliever earned the save by retiring the side.
The closer shut down the opposition for the save.
The lefty reliever came in for the save.
A double play sealed the save for the pitcher.

senses.definition: (baseball) a baserunner being tagged out while attempting to steal a base

examples.x: The catcher made a perfect throw to get the runner and recorded a caught stealing.
His attempt to steal second ended in a caught stealing after a quick tag by the shortstop.
The pitcher's pick-off move resulted in a crucial caught stealing at first base.
The speedy runner was hesitant to attempt a steal after witnessing several caught stealings earlier in the game.
The baserunner's aggressive attempt led to a caught stealing, ending the inning.

senses.definition: (in baseball) to return to and touch a base after a fly ball is caught before attempting to advance to the next base

examples.x: He had to tag up at second base before running to third.
The runner tagged up and dashed home to score.
She quickly tagged up and then sprinted to the next base.
The coach reminded the players to tag up on fly balls.

senses.definition: (in baseball) to touch a runner with the ball to get them out

examples.x: The shortstop tagged out the baserunner sliding into second.
The infielder tagged out the player attempting to steal second.
The outfielder tagged out the runner.

senses.definition: a part of the baseball game where each team gets a turn to bat and field

examples.x: The team scored three runs in the first inning.
He pitched a perfect inning, retiring all three batters.
They made a crucial error in the ninth inning.
The team rallied in the final inning to win the game.

senses.definition: (baseball) a list showing the order of batters for a team

examples.x: The manager filled out the card with the batting order.
His name was added to the starting card for the first time.
The card showed who would be up to bat next.
The team's best hitter was listed third on the card.
They made a last-minute change to the card.

senses.definition: (baseball) the path a baserunner must follow while running from one base to another

examples.x: The runner stayed close to the baseline to avoid being tagged out.
He rounded the baseline after hitting a single.
The coach instructed the player to run the baseline aggressively.
She slid into second base along the baseline.
The umpire watched closely to ensure the runner stayed within the baseline.

senses.definition: the act of a batter attempting to hit the ball with the bat in baseball

examples.x: He took a powerful swing at the pitch but missed.
She adjusted her swing to hit the curve.
His swing was quick and decisive, resulting in a line drive.
The batter's swing connected with the ball, sending it deep into center field.
The batter's swing was late on the fastball.

senses.definition: a swing and miss by the batter, where the bat fails to make contact with the ball

examples.x: He swung hard but had a whiff on the high fastball.
The batter's whiff on the curveball gave the pitcher an advantage.
She avoided a whiff by laying off the low pitch.
The hitter's whiff on the changeup was evident.
The umpire signaled strike three after the batter's whiff.

senses.definition: a base hit that allows the batter to reach base safely without being put out by the defense

examples.x: The outfielder misjudged the fly ball, resulting in a safety.
His safety sparked a rally in the late innings.
The safety cleared the bases with runners in scoring position.
The pitcher gave up back-to-back safeties in the sixth inning.
The catcher's throw to first was late, allowing the batter to reach on a safety.

senses.definition: a score resulted from a player successfully reaches home plate after touching all the bases in order without being put out

examples.x: The runner's speed allowed him to score a run from first base on a double.
She crossed the plate to score the winning run in the ninth inning.
The team celebrated after scoring five runs in the third inning.
His run tied the game in the bottom of the seventh.
The player slid safely into home to score the first run of the game.

senses.definition: (in baseball) the act of throwing the ball towards the batter in order to start the game
a statistic that shows how often a baseball player gets a hit when at bat

examples.x: She has the highest batting average on the team this season.
A high batting average can lead to more opportunities in the lineup.
Her batting average dropped after a tough week of games.
The player’s batting average reflects his consistency at the plate.

senses.definition: to deliver a baseball to the batter from the pitcher's mound with the intention of starting a play or attempting to strike out the batter

examples.x: The pitcher wound up and pitched a fastball straight down the middle of the plate.
He carefully studied the batter's stance before pitching a curveball that broke sharply.
She confidently pitched a changeup, fooling the batter with the sudden decrease in speed.
With two strikes, the pitcher decided to pitch a high fastball to try and get the batter to chase.
Despite the pressure, the rookie pitcher remained composed and pitched a perfect inning.

senses.definition: (baseball) a quick, accurate throw to a base to get a runner out

examples.x: The outfielder's peg to home plate prevented the runner from scoring.
The third baseman's quick peg to second resulted in a double play.
The shortstop made a perfect peg to first base to get the runner out.
The fielder's peg was so accurate that the runner didn't have a chance.
The center fielder's peg to third base stopped the runner from advancing.

senses.definition: (of a batter in baseball) to advance to first base by not swinging at four pitches that are outside the strike zone

examples.x: He walked during his first time at bat.
The batter showed great discipline by walking instead of swinging at those pitches.
He walked twice in that game, which helped his team get runners on base.

senses.definition: (in baseball) the elimination of a player by the opposing team, which ends their turn to bat or run
